acceptanceCriteria: Add fastapi>=0.110, uvicorn>=0.27 (NOT uvicorn[standard] — uvloop fails on Windows), websockets>=12.0, jinja2>=3.1 to pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]
Add httpx>=0.27 to pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies] dev list
Add [tool.setuptools.package-data] to pyproject.toml: quant_scanner = ['templates/**/*.html', 'static/**/*'] so templates/static are included in wheel builds
Run pip install -e '.[dev]' to install all new packages in the existing .venv
Create src/quant_scanner/server.py with FastAPI app instance: app = FastAPI(title='Crypto Quant Scanner', version='0.1.0')

Implement async lifespan context manager using @asynccontextmanager pattern. For now it is a no-op: record start_time using time.monotonic() at startup, yield, do nothing at shutdown. Store start_time on app.state.start_time

Implement GET /api/health route returning JSON: {status: 'ok', version: '0.1.0', uptime_seconds: float} where uptime_seconds = time.monotonic() - start_time. Use time.monotonic() for uptime (immune to NTP clock sync jumps)
Implement GET /favicon.ico route returning 204 No Content (prevents 404 log spam from browsers)

Create tests/test_server.py using httpx.AsyncClient as async context manager with httpx.ASGITransport(app=app) to properly trigger lifespan events: async with httpx.AsyncClient(transport=..., base_url='http://test') as client:
Test test_health_endpoint: GET /api/health returns status 200 with JSON body containing 'status' == 'ok'
Test test_health_has_uptime: response JSON contains 'uptime_seconds' key with value >= 0
Test test_health_has_version: response JSON contains 'version' matching quant_scanner.__version__
Test test_favicon_no_404: GET /favicon.ico returns 204 (not 404)
All 48 existing tests still pass unchanged
stories.gateCmd: cd /f/TradingScanner && .venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/test_server.py tests/test_scaffold.py tests/test_math.py tests/test_ingestion.py tests/test_screener.py tests/test_cli.py -v

acceptanceCriteria: Create src/quant_scanner/scan_store.py with class ScanStore. Add docstring: 'Coroutine-safe (asyncio.Lock), NOT thread-safe. All access on single event loop.'

Constructor takes max_history: int = 288 (24h at 5min intervals). Initialize: _latest = None, _history = [], _scan_count = 0, _status = 'idle', _error = None, _last_scan_at = None, _next_scan_at = None, _lock = asyncio.Lock()

async def update(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> None: acquire lock, store df.copy() as _latest (COPY to prevent caller mutation), record updated_at timestamp (UTC), append snapshot summary {timestamp, coin_count, top_beta_symbol} to _history, trim to max_history, increment _scan_count, set _last_scan_at
def get_latest(self) -> pd.DataFrame | None: return self._latest.copy() if self._latest is not None else None. MUST return a copy to prevent route handlers from mutating stored data

def get_latest_as_records(self) -> list[dict]: CRITICAL for JSON serialization. Convert NaN to None before returning: df.where(df.notna(), None).to_dict(orient='records'). This prevents invalid JSON (NaN is not valid JSON per RFC 8259). Return [] if no latest
def get_history(self, limit: int = 24) -> list[dict]: return last N snapshot summaries

def get_status(self) -> dict: return {last_scan_at, next_scan_at, scan_count, coin_count, status, error}. Note: uptime_seconds is NOT here — it belongs only in /api/health computed from app.state.start_time
def set_error(self, error: str) -> None: set status='error' and error message
def clear_error(self) -> None: set status='idle' and error=None
def set_scanning(self) -> None: set status='scanning'
def set_next_scan_at(self, dt: datetime) -> None: update next_scan_at field
Create tests/test_scan_store.py with ALL of these tests:
test_initial_state: new store has None latest, empty history, status='idle', scan_count=0
test_update_stores_dataframe: after update(), get_latest() returns df with same length and columns but is a DIFFERENT object (copy check: id(stored) != id(original))
test_update_records_history: after 3 updates with different dfs, get_history(3) returns 3 entries with correct coin_counts
test_history_limit: create store with max_history=5, do 8 updates, verify len(get_history(10)) == 5
test_status_after_update: scan_count increments by 1 per update, coin_count matches latest df length, last_scan_at is not None
test_error_handling: set_error('API down') sets status='error' and error='API down'; clear_error() resets to status='idle' and error=None
test_concurrent_updates: use asyncio.gather to run 10 update() calls simultaneously, verify scan_count == 10 and no corruption
test_empty_dataframe_update: update with empty df (pd.DataFrame()) sets coin_count=0, still records in history with coin_count=0
test_snapshot_contains_top_beta: update with df containing beta column, verify history entry has top_beta_symbol matching the symbol with highest beta
test_get_latest_returns_copy: get_latest() returns a new copy each time; modifying the returned df does not affect the store

test_get_latest_as_records_nan_safe: update with df containing np.nan in circulating_pct, call get_latest_as_records(), verify result is a list of dicts with None (not NaN) for the nan field, and json.dumps(result) succeeds without error
Create src/quant_scanner/scheduler.py with class ScanScheduler

Constructor takes: store (ScanStore), interval_seconds (int, default=300), scan_kwargs (dict|None, default=None). If scan_kwargs is None, default to {'use_cache': True}. Also accepts optional ws_manager (ConnectionManager|None, default=None) for WebSocket broadcasts. Initialize _scanning = False, _task = None, _trigger_event = asyncio.Event(), _last_trigger_time = 0
async def start(self) -> None: if already running (self._task is not None and not done), log warning and return (idempotent). Otherwise create asyncio.Task for self._loop()
async def stop(self) -> None: if self._task exists, cancel it, await with 30s timeout via asyncio.wait_for(). On TimeoutError, log warning. On CancelledError, pass (expected). Set self._task = None

async def trigger_now(self) -> None: if self._scanning is True, do nothing and return. Apply 10-second cooldown: if time.monotonic() - self._last_trigger_time < 10, do nothing (prevents /api/refresh spam). Otherwise set self._trigger_event and record trigger time

async def _loop(self) -> None: infinite loop: record scan_start = time.monotonic(), call _run_one_scan(), compute remaining = max(0, interval - (time.monotonic() - scan_start)), set store.set_next_scan_at(), then wait for EITHER asyncio.sleep(remaining) OR self._trigger_event using asyncio.wait with FIRST_COMPLETED. On CancelledError, exit cleanly. Clear the event after waking. This prevents scan-duration drift.

async def _run_one_scan(self) -> None: set self._scanning = True and store.set_scanning(). Import run_screen from screener_engine LAZILY inside the method (not at module top) to avoid circular imports and ensure --dry-run works without importing server. Call await run_screen(**self.scan_kwargs) inside try/except. On success: await store.update(result), store.clear_error(), if ws_manager: await ws_manager.broadcast({'type': 'scan_complete', 'coin_count': len(result)}). On ANY exception: store.set_error(str(e)), logger.exception('Scan failed'). Finally: self._scanning = False

Create tests/test_scheduler.py with ALL tests mocking run_screen via unittest.mock.patch — ZERO network calls. Use asyncio.Event for synchronization instead of asyncio.sleep to prevent flaky timing-dependent tests:

Create a pytest fixture with cleanup to prevent leaked background tasks: @pytest.fixture async def scheduler(store): sched = ScanScheduler(store=store, interval_seconds=9999); yield sched; await sched.stop()
test_scheduler_calls_run_screen: mock run_screen to set an asyncio.Event when called, start scheduler with trigger_now(), await the event, verify run_screen was called
test_scheduler_stores_result: mock run_screen returns a valid DataFrame, trigger scan, verify store.get_latest() is not None and matches expected coin count

test_scheduler_survives_exception: mock run_screen to raise RuntimeError('API down'), trigger scan, verify store status is 'error' with error message containing 'API down', AND verify scheduler didn't crash (trigger another scan that succeeds)
test_scheduler_stop_clean: start then immediately stop, verify self._task is None or done
test_trigger_now: start scheduler with interval=9999 (very long), immediately call trigger_now(), await mock event, verify run_screen was called (proving it didn't wait for the long interval)
test_scheduler_start_idempotent: start() twice, verify only one background task exists (not two)

test_scan_sets_status: mock run_screen with an asyncio.Event to control timing — hold the event to keep 'scanning' state observable, verify store.get_status()['status'] == 'scanning' during scan, then release and verify 'idle' after
test_scheduler_passes_kwargs: pass scan_kwargs={'exchange_id': 'kraken', 'min_beta': 2.0}, verify run_screen was called with those kwargs

Update src/quant_scanner/server.py lifespan to: create ScanStore, create ScanScheduler (with store), start scheduler at startup, stop scheduler at shutdown. Store on app.state: app.state.store, app.state.scheduler

Add module-level _DEFAULT_CONFIG dict and _server_config dict (copy of defaults): scan_kwargs={}, interval_seconds=300. Add configure() function that updates _server_config. Lifespan reads from _server_config when creating scheduler. Export _DEFAULT_CONFIG for test cleanup
Configure Jinja2Templates pointing to the templates directory. Use pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / 'templates' for the path. If directory doesn't exist, log warning but don't crash
Mount StaticFiles at /static pointing to the static directory. Use pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / 'static'. If directory doesn't exist, skip mounting (don't crash)
Create templates/ and static/ directories under src/quant_scanner/ (empty placeholder files are OK for now — DASH-005 fills them in)

Create src/quant_scanner/templates/index.html as a minimal valid HTML5 page with: title 'Crypto Quant Scanner', a div#status-bar with hx-get='/partials/status' hx-trigger='every 10s', a div#results-table with hx-get='/partials/table' hx-trigger='every 60s', HTMX loaded from CDN with local vendor fallback

Create src/quant_scanner/templates/partials/table.html: renders a <table> from a 'results' context variable (list of dicts). Columns: Rank, Symbol, Name, Market Cap, 24h Volume, Beta, Correlation, Kelly %, Circ. %. If results is empty, show 'Waiting for first scan...' or 'No coins pass current filters' based on 'has_scanned' context bool
Create src/quant_scanner/templates/partials/status.html: renders status badge, last_scan_at, next_scan_at, coin_count, scan_count from a 'status' context variable

Implement route GET / that renders index.html with context: results=store.get_latest_as_records(), status=store.get_status(), has_scanned=(store.get_latest() is not None), config=_server_config['scan_kwargs']

Implement route GET /partials/table that renders partials/table.html with results context. Add Jinja2 filters for number formatting: format_mcap (45000000 → '$45.0M', None → 'N/A', NaN → 'N/A'), format_volume (same), format_pct (0.78 → '78.0%', None/NaN → 'N/A'), format_beta (2.34 → '2.34', None/NaN → 'N/A'). All filters MUST handle None and NaN without crashing
Implement route GET /partials/status that renders partials/status.html with status context. Include relative time formatting (seconds ago → '2m ago', '1h ago')

Implement route GET /api/scan returning JSON: {results: store.get_latest_as_records(), count: int, scanned_at: str|null, status: str}. CRITICAL: Use get_latest_as_records() NOT df.to_dict() — NaN is invalid JSON
Implement route GET /api/history returning JSON: list of snapshot dicts from store.get_history()
Implement route POST /api/refresh that calls scheduler.trigger_now() and returns 202 with {message: 'Scan triggered'}
Update GET /api/health to include scanner status from store.get_status()
Add autouse test fixture to tests/test_server.py that resets _server_config to _DEFAULT_CONFIG before and after each test to prevent test pollution
Add tests to tests/test_server.py (all using httpx.AsyncClient as async context manager, all mocking run_screen):
test_index_returns_html: GET / returns 200 with text/html content type
test_index_contains_htmx: response body contains 'htmx.org'
test_partials_table_returns_200: GET /partials/table returns 200
test_partials_status_returns_200: GET /partials/status returns 200
test_api_scan_json_structure: GET /api/scan returns JSON with 'results', 'count', 'scanned_at', 'status' keys
test_api_scan_empty_before_first_scan: before any scan runs, GET /api/scan returns count=0 and results=[]

test_api_history_returns_list: GET /api/history returns 200 with JSON list
test_api_refresh_returns_202: POST /api/refresh returns 202
test_health_includes_scanner_status: GET /api/health response includes 'scanner' key with 'status' field
test_format_mcap_none: Jinja2 format_mcap filter returns 'N/A' for None input
test_format_mcap_nan: Jinja2 format_mcap filter returns 'N/A' for NaN input

stories.description: Frontend Styling, Color Coding & Chart Placeholder
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/quant_scanner/templates/index.html to full production layout: header with title + live indicator (connection status dot), status bar section, config display showing active exchange/thresholds, controls row with refresh button (hx-post='/api/refresh'), results table section, footer with version and attribution

Load HTMX from CDN with local fallback: <script src='https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2.0.4'></script> then <script>window.htmx || document.write('<script src="/static/vendor/htmx.min.js"><\/script>')</script>. Same pattern for Lightweight Charts
Download htmx.min.js (v2.0.4) and lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js (v4.2.2) into src/quant_scanner/static/vendor/ as local fallbacks (~60KB total)
Include HTMX hx-indicator='.spinner' on table and status containers. Add a .spinner element (hidden by default, shown during HTMX requests)
Add error handling on HTMX requests: hx-on::after-request attribute that shows 'Error loading data' if request failed, preserving existing content

Update src/quant_scanner/templates/partials/table.html with full styling: each metric cell gets a CSS class based on value — beta-high (>2.0), beta-mid (1.5-2.0), beta-low; corr-high (>0.85), corr-mid (0.7-0.85), corr-low; kelly-high (>0.15), kelly-mid (>0), kelly-zero. These classes are set via Jinja2 conditionals
Number formatting in table.html: Market Cap as '$XX.XM' or '$X.XXB', Volume same pattern, Beta as 'X.XX', Correlation as 'X.XX', Kelly as 'XX.X%', Circ. Supply as 'XX.X%' or 'N/A' for NaN/None

Update src/quant_scanner/templates/partials/status.html with: colored status badge (green dot for idle, yellow for scanning, red for error), relative time for last scan ('2m ago'), countdown for next scan, error message display in red if present

Create src/quant_scanner/static/style.css with dark theme: background #0d1117, text #c9d1d9, table with alternating row colors (#161b22 / #0d1117), hover highlight (#21262d), header styling, status bar layout

CSS color classes: .beta-high { color: #3fb950; font-weight: bold; }, .beta-mid { color: #d29922; }, .corr-high { color: #3fb950; font-weight: bold; }, .corr-mid { color: #d29922; }, .kelly-high { color: #3fb950; font-weight: bold; }, .kelly-mid { color: #d29922; }, .kelly-zero { color: #484f58; }
CSS responsive: table container scrolls horizontally on screens < 768px
CSS spinner: .spinner { display: none; } .htmx-request .spinner { display: inline-block; } with a CSS animation
CSS transitions: .htmx-swapping { opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.2s; }

Create src/quant_scanner/static/charts.js as a placeholder: single function initCharts() that logs 'Charts initialized' to console. Wire into index.html with DOMContentLoaded listener. Leave a comment '// TODO: Implement sparkline charts with Lightweight Charts library'
Add tests to tests/test_server.py:
test_index_contains_table_tag: GET / response contains '<table'
test_index_loads_stylesheet: GET / response contains '/static/style.css'
test_static_css_served: GET /static/style.css returns 200 with text/css content type
test_partials_table_has_color_classes: after mocking a scan with beta=2.5, GET /partials/table response contains 'beta-high'
test_partials_table_empty_message: before any scan, GET /partials/table contains 'Waiting for first scan'
stories.gateCmd: cd /f/TradingScanner && .venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/test_server.py -v && .venv/Scripts/python -c "from quant_scanner.server import app; print('Server imports OK')"

acceptanceCriteria: Create src/quant_scanner/ws_manager.py with class ConnectionManager
Constructor initializes self._connections: set[WebSocket] = set()
async def connect(self, websocket: WebSocket) -> None: await websocket.accept(), add to set, log at DEBUG level
def disconnect(self, websocket: WebSocket) -> None: discard from set (safe if not present), log at DEBUG level

async def broadcast(self, message: dict) -> None: iterate over copy of connections set, send_json to each. If send raises any exception, catch it, call disconnect() on that websocket, continue to next. Log broadcast at DEBUG level
def client_count(self) -> int: return len of active connections
Update src/quant_scanner/server.py: create ConnectionManager instance, store on app.state.ws_manager, pass to ScanScheduler at init
Add WS /ws/updates route: accept connection via manager.connect(), loop receiving messages (keep-alive), on WebSocketDisconnect call manager.disconnect()

Update scheduler._run_one_scan(): after successful scan + store.update(), if ws_manager is not None, await ws_manager.broadcast({'type': 'scan_complete', 'coin_count': len(result), 'scanned_at': ISO timestamp})

Update templates/index.html JavaScript section (ONLY the <script> block, not the HTML structure from DASH-005): on DOMContentLoaded, connect to ws://${location.host}/ws/updates. On message with type=='scan_complete', trigger HTMX refresh via JS: htmx.trigger('#results-table', 'htmx:abort') then htmx.ajax('GET', '/partials/table', '#results-table'). This replaces the table content via JS, NOT via a second hx-trigger attribute — eliminates the double-refresh race condition
WebSocket auto-reconnect in JS: on close/error, reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s). Show connection indicator (green dot = connected, gray dot = reconnecting)
Update GET /api/health to include websocket_clients count from manager.client_count()
Create tests/test_ws_manager.py (unit tests with mock WebSocket objects):
test_connect_adds_to_set: mock WebSocket, call connect, verify client_count() == 1
test_disconnect_removes: connect then disconnect, verify client_count() == 0
test_disconnect_idempotent: disconnect a websocket that was never connected (or already disconnected), no error raised
test_broadcast_sends_to_all: connect 2 mock websockets, broadcast a message, verify send_json called on both
test_broadcast_handles_dead_client: connect 2 websockets, make one raise Exception on send_json, broadcast, verify other client still received message and dead client was removed
test_client_count: connect 3, disconnect 1, verify client_count() == 2
test_broadcast_to_zero_clients: broadcast with empty connections set, no error raised

WebSocket endpoint integration test in test_server.py using Starlette TestClient (NOT httpx — httpx cannot test WebSocket): from starlette.testclient import TestClient; test_ws_endpoint_connects: with TestClient(app) as client: with client.websocket_connect('/ws/updates') as ws: pass

Update cli.py parse_args(): add --serve (action='store_true', default=False, help='Launch live web dashboard'), --port (type=int, default=8080), --host (type=str, default='127.0.0.1'), --refresh-interval (type=int, default=300, help='Scan refresh interval in seconds')

Update cli.py main(): if args.serve AND args.dry_run both set, print error message 'Cannot use --serve and --dry-run together' and sys.exit(1). If args.serve: LAZILY import server and uvicorn (inside the if block, not at module top — this ensures --dry-run works even if FastAPI is somehow missing), build scan_kwargs dict from args (exchange_id, min_mcap, max_mcap, min_beta, min_correlation=args.min_corr, min_volume, use_cache=not args.no_cache), call server.configure(scan_kwargs=scan_kwargs, interval_seconds=args.refresh_interval), wrap uvicorn.run() in try/except OSError to catch port-in-use and print friendly message
Existing --dry-run behavior completely unchanged — verify --dry-run does NOT import server or uvicorn modules
Existing one-shot scan (no flags) behavior completely unchanged
Update tests/test_cli.py:
test_serve_flag: parse_args(['--serve']).serve is True
test_default_serve_false: parse_args([]).serve is False
test_port_flag: parse_args(['--port', '9090']).port == 9090
test_default_port: parse_args([]).port == 8080
test_host_flag: parse_args(['--host', '0.0.0.0']).host == '0.0.0.0'
test_default_host: parse_args([]).host == '127.0.0.1'
test_refresh_interval_flag: parse_args(['--refresh-interval', '60']).refresh_interval == 60
test_default_refresh_interval: parse_args([]).refresh_interval == 300
Existing test_dry_run_flag and test_default_args still pass
The command 'python -m quant_scanner --dry-run' still produces the Rich table and exits with code 0

Verify graceful shutdown: FastAPI lifespan cleanup calls scheduler.stop() which cancels the background task with 30s timeout. If timeout, log warning but don't hang. All WebSocket connections are closed on shutdown. NOTE: On Windows, only SIGINT (Ctrl+C) triggers shutdown — SIGTERM is not supported on Windows
Verify memory safety: ScanStore history bounded at max_history. Old DataFrames are replaced (via .copy()) not accumulated. WebSocket manager prunes dead connections on broadcast
Add structured logging at server startup: log bind address, port, refresh interval, exchange, all threshold values using logger.info(). Add uvicorn access logging for HTTP request visibility
Each scan logged: 'Scan started', 'Scan complete: N coins in X.Xs' or 'Scan failed: error message'
Enhanced /api/health returns: {status, version, uptime_seconds, scanner: {status, last_scan_at, scan_count, coin_count, error}, websocket_clients: int}
Create tests/test_integration_dashboard.py with ALL tests mocking run_screen — ZERO network calls:

test_full_lifecycle: create app with mocked run_screen returning 3 coins, use httpx.AsyncClient as async context manager → GET /api/health returns ok → trigger scan → GET /api/scan returns 3 results → GET /partials/table contains expected symbol names → GET /partials/status shows 'idle'

test_error_recovery: mock run_screen to raise on first call, succeed on second → trigger first scan → verify GET /api/scan shows error status → trigger second scan → verify GET /api/scan shows results and error is cleared
test_manual_refresh: start app with very long interval (9999s), POST /api/refresh → wait briefly → verify GET /api/scan now has results (proving manual trigger worked without waiting for interval)
test_empty_scan_result: mock run_screen returns empty DataFrame → GET /api/scan returns count=0 → GET /partials/table contains 'No coins' message → status is 'idle' NOT 'error'
test_concurrent_requests: fire 20 simultaneous GET /api/scan requests via asyncio.gather, all return 200 (no race condition crashes)
test_nan_in_scan_produces_valid_json: mock run_screen with df containing NaN circulating_pct → GET /api/scan → json.loads(response.text) succeeds (no NaN literal in output)
test_first_scan_shows_scanning_status: start app, mock run_screen with a delay, GET /partials/status during scan shows 'scanning' badge
ALL original 48 tests pass: run full pytest suite
ALL new dashboard tests pass: total test count should be approximately 106+
The command 'python -m quant_scanner --dry-run' still works exactly as before
